spec_match.rationale: The simulation engine contains a sequential head-of-line blocking bug. Because the dispatch loop is written as a sequential 'for' loop awaiting each index in-order, in 'out-of-order' mode, the simulation blocks chunk 4 (800ms) from executing/logging until chunk 3 (1400ms) completes its await.

interactive.rationale: Highly interactive: lets the user specify the delayed chunk index, delay duration, and interval ms, with dynamic progress tracks and interactive mode switches.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Matches the description of letting users pick delay offsets and visually inspect logs.

edge_cases.rationale: Validates input numbers (min/max limits) and manages button disabling states safely.

references.rationale: The page has a see also links section but completely omits the mandatory external ChromeStatus link.

design_system.rationale: Fits the repository design perfectly, with crisp panel layouts, interactive input fields, and distinct console logging colors.

openQuestions.title: Fix sequential dispatch engine bug
openQuestions.detail: The simulation loop on line 329 executes sequentially. In out-of-order mode, a delayed chunk N blocks the execution loop from reaching chunks N+1...TOTAL_CHUNKS, delaying their logs. Rewrite the simulation to schedule independent timers or sort the execution array by arrival times to ensure true asynchronous out-of-order execution.

openQuestions.title: Cite ChromeStatus link under see also
openQuestions.detail: Add the canonical link to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5111042975465472 in the see also reference links section at the bottom of the page.
